<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Model theory</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>

<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */

← Notebook
1 Structures 2 Elementary equivalence 3 Iso vs ≡ 4 Types & saturation 5 EF games 6 Applications

Model theory

Where syntax meets the structures it describes. First-order logic gives us a language; model theory studies what that language can — and crucially, cannot — see about groups, orders, fields, and graphs. By the end you'll know why $(\mathbb{Q},<)$ and $(\mathbb{R},<)$ look identical to first-order logic, why connectivity isn't first-order, and how compactness gives Ax–Grothendieck for free.

Logic supplies sentences; structures supply the worlds they describe. Two structures may be very different objects yet satisfy exactly the same first-order sentences — they are elementarily equivalent. Or they may look superficially similar yet be separated by a single sentence of low quantifier rank. Model theory is the systematic study of this gap: what does first-order logic see, what does it miss, and what tools — types, saturation, Ehrenfeucht–Fraïssé games, transfer principles — let us compute the answer.

Plan. §1 defines structures and signatures. §2 introduces elementary equivalence — first-order indistinguishability. §3 contrasts it with isomorphism: when do they coincide, when do they part? §4 introduces types and saturation, the local-to-global engine of the subject. §5 gives EF games as a combinatorial test for elementary equivalence. §6 collects three applications to algebra: completeness of ACF, the Lefschetz principle, and Ax–Grothendieck.

blocks.html:
1. Structures and signatures

A signature (or language) $\sigma$ is a list of symbols, each labelled with an arity:

  • Constant symbols $c$ (arity $0$).
  • Function symbols $f$ of arity $n\ge 1$.
  • Relation symbols $R$ of arity $n\ge 1$.

A $\sigma$-structure $\mathfrak{A}$ is a non-empty set $A$ (the domain) together with an interpretation of every symbol: each constant $c$ gets an element $c^{\mathfrak{A}}\in A$, each $n$-ary function $f$ gets a function $f^{\mathfrak{A}}\colon A^n\to A$, each $n$-ary relation $R$ gets a subset $R^{\mathfrak{A}}\subseteq A^n$. The signature is the grammar; the structure is the world the grammar names.

The standard zoo.
  • Group: $\sigma_{\mathrm{gp}} = \{e, \cdot, ^{-1}\}$ — constant, binary, unary.
  • Order: $\sigma_{<} = \{<\}$ — one binary relation.
  • Ring: $\sigma_{\mathrm{rg}} = \{0, 1, +, \cdot, -\}$ — two constants, two binary, one unary.
  • Graph: $\sigma_{\mathrm{gr}} = \{E\}$ — one binary relation, usually symmetric and irreflexive.
The same domain can host different structures depending on which signature you read it through — $(\mathbb{Z}, +, \cdot, 0, 1)$ is a ring, $(\mathbb{Z}, <)$ is a discrete order, $(\mathbb{Z}, +)$ is a group.

The widget below shows you a tiny domain $A=\{1,2,3,4\}$ and lets you swap signatures. Toggle constants and edit the function tables or relation matrix; the readout previews a few atomic formulas evaluated under the current interpretation.

Signature explorer
pick a signature; click cells to edit; readout shows atomic-formula truths

Used in

<script>
/* =========================== Signature explorer =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#st-svg'), out=$('#st-out'), sigSel=$('#st-sig');
  const randBtn=$('#st-rand'), emptyBtn=$('#st-empty');
  const N = 4;                  // domain {1,2,3,4}
  let sig = 'rel';
  // {R}: an N×N 0/1 matrix
  let R = Array.from({length:N},()=>Array(N).fill(0));
  // {f,c}: f is an array A→A; c is an integer in {0..N-1}
  let f = [1,2,3,0]; let c = 0;
  // ord starts as the natural order

  function reshape(){
    if(sig==='rel'){
      R = Array.from({length:N},(_,i)=>Array.from({length:N},(_,j)=> i!==j && Math.random()<0.45 ? 1 : 0));
    } else if(sig==='fn'){
      f = Array.from({length:N},()=>Math.floor(Math.random()*N));
      c = Math.floor(Math.random()*N);
    } else {
      // ord: keep the natural strict order
      R = Array.from({length:N},(_,i)=>Array.from({length:N},(_,j)=> i<j ? 1 : 0));
    }
  }
  function setEmpty(){
    if(sig==='rel') R = Array.from({length:N},()=>Array(N).fill(0));
    else if(sig==='fn'){ f = [0,0,0,0]; c = 0; }
    else R = Array.from({length:N},()=>Array(N).fill(0));
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=240;
    const pad = 24;
    if(sig==='rel' || sig==='ord'){
      // matrix on the left
      const cell = 28;
      const X0 = pad+30, Y0 = pad+30;
      // headers
      for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        const t = SVG('text',{x:X0+j*cell+cell/2,y:Y0-8,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
        t.textContent = String(j+1); svg.appendChild(t);
      }
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const t = SVG('text',{x:X0-10,y:Y0+i*cell+cell/2+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
        t.textContent = String(i+1); svg.appendChild(t);
      }
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++) for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        const fill = R[i][j] ? 'var(--cyan)' : 'var(--panel2)';
        svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:X0+j*cell, y:Y0+i*cell, width:cell-2, height:cell-2,
          fill, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1, rx:3,
          style:'cursor:pointer','data-i':String(i),'data-j':String(j)}));
      }
      const lbl = SVG('text',{x:X0+N*cell/2,y:Y0-26,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      lbl.textContent = sig==='ord' ? 'order matrix (cyan = i < j)' : 'relation R (cyan = R(i,j) holds)';
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
      // graph view on the right
      const Gx=400, Gy=120, radius=68;
      const pos=[];
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const ang = -Math.PI/2 + 2*Math.PI*i/N;
        pos.push([Gx+radius*Math.cos(ang), Gy+radius*Math.sin(ang)]);
      }
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++) for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        if(!R[i][j] || i===j) continue;
        drawArrow(svg,pos[i],pos[j],{color:'var(--violet)',curve:14,markerId:'st-arr',width:1.4,pad1:18,pad2:20});
      }
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++) drawNode(svg,pos[i][0],pos[i][1],String(i+1),{r:13,stroke:'var(--cyan)'});
      const gl = SVG('text',{x:Gx,y:Gy+radius+30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      gl.textContent = 'graph view';
      svg.appendChild(gl);
    } else {
      // {f,c}: a function-graph with arrows i → f(i)
      // bullets in a row, arrows above
      const X0 = 80, Y0 = 130, dx = 90;
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        // draw arrows underneath nodes
        const fi = f[i];
        const sx = X0+i*dx, tx = X0+fi*dx;
        const sy = Y0, ty = Y0;
        if(i===fi){
          // self-loop
          svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:`M${sx-7} ${sy-12} A 12 16 0 1 1 ${sx+7} ${sy-12}`,
            fill:'none', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.4,'marker-end':'url(#st-arr-fn)'}));
          ensureArrow(svg,'st-arr-fn','var(--violet)');
        } else {
          drawArrow(svg,[sx,sy-12],[tx,ty-12],{color:'var(--violet)',curve: i<fi? -22 : 22, markerId:'st-arr-fn',width:1.4,pad1:8,pad2:10});
        }
      }
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const x = X0+i*dx;
        const hl = i===c;
        drawNode(svg,x,Y0,String(i+1),{r:18, stroke: hl?'var(--yellow)':'var(--cyan)', fill: hl?'rgba(255,216,102,0.18)':'var(--panel2)'});
        if(hl){
          const cl = SVG('text',{x,y:Y0+34,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)','font-weight':700});
          cl.textContent = 'c';
          svg.appendChild(cl);
        }
      }
      const lbl1 = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:60,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      lbl1.textContent = 'click a node: cycle f(node) to next; shift-click = move c';
      svg.appendChild(lbl1);
      const lbl2 = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:200,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      lbl2.textContent = 'function graph: violet arrows are i ↦ f(i); yellow ring marks c';
      svg.appendChild(lbl2);
    }
    renderReadout();
  }

  function renderReadout(){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`domain  A = {1, 2, 3, 4}`);
    if(sig==='rel'){
      let cnt=0; for(let i=0;i<N;i++) for(let j=0;j<N;j++) if(R[i][j]) cnt++;
      lines.push(`signature σ = {R}    |R| = ${cnt} ordered pairs`);
      lines.push('');
      // a few atomic formulas
      const refl = R.every((row,i)=>row[i]===1);
      let sym=true; outerSym: for(let i=0;i<N;i++) for(let j=0;j<N;j++) if(R[i][j] && !R[j][i]){ sym=false; break outerSym; }
      const r12 = R[0][1]?'true':'false';
      const r34 = R[2][3]?'true':'false';
      lines.push(`A ⊨ R(1,2)                   : ${r12}`);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ R(3,4)                   : ${r34}`);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀x R(x,x)                : ${refl?'true':'false'}`);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀x∀y(R(x,y)→R(y,x))      : ${sym?'true':'false'}`);
    } else if(sig==='fn'){
      lines.push(`signature σ = {f, c}    f: A→A    c ∈ A`);
      lines.push(`f = [${f.map(x=>x+1).join(', ')}]    c = ${c+1}`);
      lines.push('');
      lines.push(`A ⊨ f(c) = c                 : ${f[c]===c ? 'true':'false'}`);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ f(f(c)) = c              : ${f[f[c]]===c ? 'true':'false'}`);
      // surjective?
      const img = new Set(f);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀y ∃x f(x) = y           : ${img.size===N ? 'true':'false'}  (f surjective)`);
      // injective?
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀x∀y(f(x)=f(y)→x=y)      : ${img.size===N ? 'true':'false'}  (f injective)`);
      lines.push(`(on a finite set, surjective = injective.)`);
    } else {
      lines.push(`signature σ = {<}`);
      let strict=true, refl=false; outerS: for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        if(R[i][i]){ refl=true; break; }
      }
      let trans=true; outerT: for(let i=0;i<N;i++) for(let j=0;j<N;j++) for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
        if(R[i][j] && R[j][k] && !R[i][k]){ trans=false; break outerT; }
      }
      let total=true; outerL: for(let i=0;i<N;i++) for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        if(i!==j && !R[i][j] && !R[j][i]){ total=false; break outerL; }
      }
      lines.push('');
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀x ¬(x < x)              : ${refl?'false':'true'}   (irreflexive)`);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀x∀y∀z(x<y∧y<z→x<z)     : ${trans?'true':'false'}   (transitive)`);
      lines.push(`A ⊨ ∀x∀y(x≠y → x<y ∨ y<x)   : ${total?'true':'false'}   (totality)`);
      lines.push('');
      lines.push(`(strict total order ⇐ irreflexive + transitive + totality.)`);
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }

  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if(sig==='rel' || sig==='ord'){
      const i = e.target?.getAttribute?.('data-i'), j = e.target?.getAttribute?.('data-j');
      if(i==null || j==null) return;
      const ii = parseInt(i,10), jj = parseInt(j,10);
      R[ii][jj] = R[ii][jj] ? 0 : 1;
      render();
    } else {
      // {f,c}: clicking a node — find which one
      const pt = svg.createSVGPoint(); pt.x = e.clientX; pt.y = e.clientY;
      const ctm = svg.getScreenCTM(); if(!ctm) return;
      const inv = ctm.inverse();
      const p = pt.matrixTransform(inv);
      const X0=80, Y0=130, dx=90;
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
        const x = X0+i*dx;
        if((p.x-x)*(p.x-x) + (p.y-Y0)*(p.y-Y0) < 22*22){
          if(e.shiftKey){ c = i; }
          else { f[i] = (f[i]+1) % N; }
          render(); return;
        }
      }
    }
  });
  sigSel.addEventListener('change', ()=>{ sig = sigSel.value; reshape(); render(); });
  randBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ reshape(); render(); });
  emptyBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ setEmpty(); render(); });
  reshape(); render();
})();
</script>

2. Elementary equivalence

Once we have structures and a logic to talk about them, the natural question is: when are two structures logically the same?

Elementary equivalence. Two $\sigma$-structures $\mathfrak{A},\mathfrak{B}$ are elementarily equivalent, written $\mathfrak{A}\equiv\mathfrak{B}$, iff for every first-order $\sigma$-sentence $\varphi$, $$\mathfrak{A}\models\varphi \;\iff\; \mathfrak{B}\models\varphi.$$ Equivalently, $\mathrm{Th}(\mathfrak{A}) = \mathrm{Th}(\mathfrak{B})$ — the structures have identical complete first-order theories.

This is much coarser than isomorphism. Cardinality is invisible (Löwenheim–Skolem); the standard model of arithmetic $\mathbb{N}$ and any non-standard model are equivalent by construction; $(\mathbb{Q},<)$ and $(\mathbb{R},<)$ are both dense linear orders without endpoints, and that theory is complete, so they too are elementarily equivalent.

Why a theory being "complete" matters. A theory $T$ is complete when, for every sentence $\varphi$, $T\vdash\varphi$ or $T\vdash\neg\varphi$. Equivalently, all models of $T$ are elementarily equivalent. Many natural theories — DLO (dense linear orders without endpoints), ACF$_p$ (algebraically closed fields of characteristic $p$), the theory of $(\mathbb{Z},+)$ — are complete. Many others — PA, ZFC, the theory of groups — are not.

The widget below presents a roster of pairs of structures and lets you probe each with a sentence. Sentences come in five quantifier-rank tiers; the tier you can find a separator at gives a quantitative measure of how close the two structures are.

pick a pair; pick a sentence; the readout reports who satisfies it

<script>
/* =========================== Equivalence prober =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#eq-svg'), out=$('#eq-out'), pairSel=$('#eq-pair'), sentSel=$('#eq-sent');
  // Each pair: name, two structures, a "side" function (i.e. for each sentence, is it true on side L / R?)
  // We hard-code truth values rather than deriving them — pedagogical not foundational.
  const PAIRS = [
    {nL:'(Q,<)', nR:'(R,<)', name:'(Q,<) vs (R,<)',
     truth:[false,true,true,true,false,true]},      // min, no max, dense, ≥2, /2 (lang has no +), ≥4
    {nL:'(N,<)', nR:'(Z,<)', name:'(N,<) vs (Z,<)',
     truthL:[true,true,false,true,false,true],
     truthR:[false,true,false,true,false,true]},
    {nL:'(Z,+)', nR:'(Q,+)', name:'(Z,+) vs (Q,+)',
     truthL:[true,true,false,true,false,true],     // (Z,+): "minimum" is meaningless; tier 0 sentence is in {<}
     truthR:[true,true,false,true,true,true]},
    {nL:'({1,2,3},<)', nR:'({1,2,3,4},<)', name:'finite orders',
     truthL:[true,false,false,true,false,false],
     truthR:[true,false,false,true,false,true]}
  ];
  // Note: sentences 0,1,2,3,5 are in the language {<}; 4 is in {+}.
  // For pair 0 we use a single truth table since both have same theory.
  const SENTENCES = [
    'has a minimum',
    'no maximum',
    'densely ordered',
    'at least 2 elements',
    'every element divisible by 2',
    'at least 4 elements'
  ];
  const SENT_LANG = ['<','<','<','<','+','<'];

  function lookup(pair, sIdx){
    if(pair.truth) return [pair.truth[sIdx], pair.truth[sIdx]];
    return [pair.truthL[sIdx], pair.truthR[sIdx]];
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=220;
    const p = PAIRS[parseInt(pairSel.value,10)];
    const s = parseInt(sentSel.value,10);
    const [tL,tR] = lookup(p,s);
    const lang = SENT_LANG[s];
    // structure language compatibility — flag if mismatch
    const langL = p.nL.includes('+') ? '+' : '<';
    const langR = p.nR.includes('+') ? '+' : '<';
    const compatL = (lang==='<' && langL==='<') || (lang==='+' && langL==='+');
    const compatR = (lang==='<' && langR==='<') || (lang==='+' && langR==='+');

    // box for L
    const boxW = 220, boxH = 110;
    const xL = 50, xR = W - 50 - boxW, y = 60;
    function drawBox(x, name, satisfies, compat){
      const fill = !compat ? 'var(--panel2)' : (satisfies ? 'rgba(131,193,103,0.13)' : 'rgba(224,122,95,0.13)');
      const stroke = !compat ? 'var(--mute)' : (satisfies ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--pink)');
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x,y,width:boxW,height:boxH,rx:10,ry:10,fill,stroke,'stroke-width':1.6}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:x+boxW/2,y:y+30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':16,
        fill:!compat?'var(--mute)':(satisfies?'var(--green)':'var(--pink)'),'font-weight':700,
        'font-family':'ui-monospace,monospace'});
      t.textContent = name;
      svg.appendChild(t);
      const v = SVG('text',{x:x+boxW/2,y:y+62,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,
        fill:!compat?'var(--mute)':(satisfies?'var(--green)':'var(--pink)')});
      v.textContent = !compat ? 'language mismatch' : (satisfies ? '⊨ φ' : '⊭ φ');
      svg.appendChild(v);
      const c = SVG('text',{x:x+boxW/2,y:y+86,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      c.textContent = !compat ? `(no ${lang} in ${name})` : (satisfies ? 'satisfies' : 'refutes');
      svg.appendChild(c);
    }
    drawBox(xL, p.nL, tL, compatL);
    drawBox(xR, p.nR, tR, compatR);

    // verdict label
    const sameVerdict = (compatL===compatR) && (tL===tR) && (compatL && compatR);
    const eq = compatL && compatR && (tL === tR);
    const txt = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:y+boxH/2+5,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,
      fill: eq ? 'var(--mute)' : 'var(--yellow)','font-weight':eq?500:700});
    txt.textContent = (compatL && compatR) ? (eq ? 'agree' : '↯ disagree') : 'language n/a';
    svg.appendChild(txt);

    const sub = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:H-30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    sub.textContent = `sentence: "${SENTENCES[s]}"  (language: ${lang})`;
    svg.appendChild(sub);
    renderReadout(p,s,tL,tR,compatL,compatR);
  }

  function renderReadout(p, s, tL, tR, compatL, compatR){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`pair      : ${p.name}`);
    lines.push(`sentence  : ${SENTENCES[s]}`);
    lines.push('');
    if(!compatL || !compatR){
      lines.push('language note: this sentence uses a symbol absent from one structure.');
      lines.push('               in model theory we always work in a fixed signature.');
      lines.push('');
    }
    lines.push(`L = ${p.nL}  : ${compatL ? (tL?'satisfies':'refutes') : 'n/a'}`);
    lines.push(`R = ${p.nR}  : ${compatR ? (tR?'satisfies':'refutes') : 'n/a'}`);
    lines.push('');
    if(compatL && compatR){
      if(tL === tR){
        lines.push(`verdict   : the two structures agree on this sentence.`);
        lines.push(`            (so this sentence cannot witness ¬(L ≡ R).)`);
      } else {
        lines.push(`verdict   : ↯  the structures disagree.`);
        lines.push(`            therefore L ≢ R — this sentence is a separator.`);
      }
    }
    if(p.name === '(Q,<) vs (R,<)'){
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('fact: DLO (dense linear orders without endpoints) is a complete theory.');
      lines.push('      every {<}-sentence has the same truth value in (Q,<) and (R,<).');
      lines.push('      (Q,<) ≡ (R,<) but they are NOT isomorphic — different cardinalities.');
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  pairSel.addEventListener('change', render);
  sentSel.addEventListener('change', render);
  render();
})();
</script>

3. Isomorphism vs elementary equivalence

Isomorphism preserves everything: every relation, every operation, every cardinality fact, every sentence — first-order or higher. Elementary equivalence preserves only first-order sentences. So $\mathfrak{A}\cong\mathfrak{B}$ implies $\mathfrak{A}\equiv\mathfrak{B}$ but not the converse.

Coincidence theorem (finite case). For finite structures over a finite signature, $\mathfrak{A}\equiv\mathfrak{B}$ iff $\mathfrak{A}\cong\mathfrak{B}$. The reason: a finite structure has a complete diagram — a single sentence describing every atomic fact about every element pair — and that sentence is first-order.

For infinite structures the gap opens up. The classical example is Cantor's back-and-forth.

Cantor's theorem (1895). Any two countable dense linear orders without endpoints are isomorphic. So $(\mathbb{Q},<) \cong $ any other countable DLO. The theory DLO is therefore $\aleph_0$-categorical: it has exactly one model up to isomorphism in cardinality $\aleph_0$.

The proof is the prototype back-and-forth construction. List both domains as $\{a_0,a_1,\ldots\}$ and $\{b_0,b_1,\ldots\}$. Build a partial isomorphism in stages. At even stages, take the smallest unmatched $a_i$ and pick any $b_j$ in $B$ that fits the order pattern (density supplies one). At odd stages, do the reverse from $B$. The union is an isomorphism.

The widget below animates this construction. Watch how at each stage a new element gets added and the partial isomorphism is extended.

Cantor's back-and-forth
click "next step" to extend the partial iso between two countable DLOs
step 0 of 8
</section>

<script>
/* =========================== Back-and-forth animation =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#bf-svg'), out=$('#bf-out');
  const prev=$('#bf-prev'), next=$('#bf-next'), reset=$('#bf-reset'), stepLbl=$('#bf-step');
  // We pre-script a sequence of 8 moves alternating "from A" and "from B".
  // Each step adds a (a_i, b_j) pair to a partial iso, with notes.
  // A and B are both copies of [0,1] ∩ Q displayed as sorted real numbers.
  const A_PICKS = [0.5, 0.2, 0.7, 0.1];     // "from A" picks
  const B_PICKS = [0.5, 0.3, 0.8, 0.6];     // "from B" picks
  // The iso pairs in order (A, B):
  const PAIRS = [
    [0.5, 0.5, 'A→B', 'pick smallest unmatched a; in B, density supplies a matching value'],
    [null, 0.3, 'B→A', 'pick smallest unmatched b; need a < (matched 0.5) in A — pick 0.2 say'],
    [0.7, null, 'A→B', 'pick next unmatched a (0.7); need b > 0.5, pick 0.8'],
    [null, 0.6, 'B→A', 'pick next unmatched b (0.6); need 0.5 < a < 0.7 in A — pick 0.6 say'],
  ];
  // Build a flat list of (label, A→B side, b-pick, a-pick)
  // We display fixed positions for simplicity: A and B intervals on top/bottom.
  const seq = [];
  for(let i=0;i<PAIRS.length;i++){
    const [a, b, dir, note] = PAIRS[i];
    seq.push({dir, note,
      pickedA: a === null ? (i===1 ? 0.2 : 0.6) : a,
      pickedB: b === null ? (i===2 ? 0.8 : 0.5) : b
    });
  }
  // Resolve the chain: cumulative iso pairs after each step.
  const pairsByStep = [];
  let acc = [];
  for(const m of seq){
    acc = acc.concat([[m.pickedA, m.pickedB]]);
    // sort by A
    acc = acc.slice().sort((p,q)=>p[0]-q[0]);
    pairsByStep.push(acc.slice());
  }
  let step = 0; // 0 = before any pair; 1 = after first; ... 4 = after 4 pairs

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=240;
    const xL = 60, xR = 560;
    const yA = 70, yB = 170;
    // top line A
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xL,y1:yA,x2:xR,y2:yA, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xL,y1:yB,x2:xR,y2:yB, stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    const ttA = SVG('text',{x:30,y:yA+5,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--cyan)','font-weight':700});
    ttA.textContent = 'A'; svg.appendChild(ttA);
    const ttB = SVG('text',{x:30,y:yB+5,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--yellow)','font-weight':700});
    ttB.textContent = 'B'; svg.appendChild(ttB);
    // tick marks 0..1
    [0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1].forEach(t=>{
      const x = xL + (xR-xL)*t;
      [yA,yB].forEach(yc=>{
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x,y1:yc-3,x2:x,y2:yc+3,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
      });
    });
    // pairs so far
    const pairs = step===0 ? [] : pairsByStep[step-1];
    for(let i=0;i<pairs.length;i++){
      const [a,b] = pairs[i];
      const ax = xL + (xR-xL)*a;
      const bx = xL + (xR-xL)*b;
      // dot on A
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:ax,cy:yA,r:5,fill:'var(--cyan)'}));
      const at = SVG('text',{x:ax,y:yA-12,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
      at.textContent = a.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(at);
      // dot on B
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:bx,cy:yB,r:5,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
      const bt = SVG('text',{x:bx,y:yB+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
      bt.textContent = b.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(bt);
      // connection
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:ax,y1:yA+4,x2:bx,y2:yB-4,
        stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-dasharray':'3 3'}));
    }
    // current move highlight
    if(step >= 1 && step <= seq.length){
      const m = seq[step-1];
      const ax = xL + (xR-xL)*m.pickedA;
      const bx = xL + (xR-xL)*m.pickedB;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:ax,cy:yA,r:9,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1.6}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:bx,cy:yB,r:9,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1.6}));
    }
    // narration
    const nt = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:H-12,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    nt.textContent = step===0 ? 'click "next" to start' :
      `step ${step}: ${seq[step-1].dir} — ${seq[step-1].note}`;
    svg.appendChild(nt);

    stepLbl.textContent = `step ${step} of ${seq.length}`;
    prev.disabled = step===0;
    next.disabled = step >= seq.length;
    prev.style.opacity = prev.disabled ? 0.5 : 1;
    next.style.opacity = next.disabled ? 0.5 : 1;
    renderReadout();
  }
  function renderReadout(){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`partial isomorphism after step ${step}:`);
    if(step===0) lines.push('  (empty)');
    else for(const [a,b] of pairsByStep[step-1]){
      lines.push(`  ${a.toFixed(2)}  ↦  ${b.toFixed(2)}`);
    }
    lines.push('');
    if(step < seq.length){
      lines.push(`next move would be: ${seq[step].dir}`);
      lines.push(`  ${seq[step].note}`);
    } else {
      lines.push('after enumerating all of A and B (countably many steps),');
      lines.push('the union is an order-preserving bijection A → B.');
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('this is Cantor\'s 1895 theorem: every countable DLO is order-isomorphic to (Q,<).');
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  next.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step<seq.length){ step++; render(); } });
  prev.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step>0){ step--; render(); } });
  reset.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ step=0; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

4. Types and saturation

Sentences talk about a structure as a whole. To talk about elements we need formulas with free variables — and to talk about possible elements (in this or any elementary extension) we need types.

Type. Fix a $\sigma$-structure $\mathfrak{A}$ and a parameter set $A_0\subseteq A$. A complete $n$-type over $A_0$ is a maximal consistent set $p(\bar x)$ of $\sigma$-formulas with parameters from $A_0$ and free variables $\bar x = (x_1,\ldots,x_n)$. The set of all complete $n$-types over $A_0$ is the type space $S_n(A_0)$.

An $n$-tuple $\bar a$ in some elementary extension realises $p$ when $\mathfrak{A}'\models\varphi(\bar a)$ for every $\varphi\in p$. By compactness, every type is realised in some elementary extension. The question is whether it is already realised in $\mathfrak{A}$ itself.

Example: a non-realised type. In $(\mathbb{Q},<)$ the set $$p(x) = \{x > q : q\in\mathbb{Q}\}$$ is consistent — every finite subset is satisfied by any rational larger than the listed $q$'s — but no rational is greater than every rational. So $p$ is omitted in $(\mathbb{Q},<)$. Compactness produces an elementary extension where it is realised — that extension contains a "point at infinity," exactly as in non-standard analysis.
Saturation. A structure $\mathfrak{A}$ is $\omega$-saturated if every type over a finite parameter set is already realised in $\mathfrak{A}$. More generally, $\mathfrak{A}$ is $\kappa$-saturated if every type over a parameter set of size $<\kappa$ is realised. Saturated models are richer than necessary — they contain witnesses to every consistent local description.

Saturation is the model-theoretic analogue of compactness in topology: it ensures that "type-theoretic limits" exist. It is the workhorse behind back-and-forth proofs (e.g., Cantor's theorem says every countable DLO is $\omega$-saturated), and behind transfer principles in §6.

The widget below visualises the type space $S_1(A_0)$ over a small parameter set in $(\mathbb{Q},<)$. Add parameters; the type space refines into intervals; some types are realised in $\mathbb{Q}$, others only in extensions.

Types over a parameter set in $(\mathbb{Q},<)$
click the line to add a parameter; type space splits into open intervals + cuts

    const real = nParams + nIntervals;     // realised types

    // labels
    const lL = SVG('text',{x:xL,y:bandY-8,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});

    lines.push('');
    lines.push(`|S₁(A₀)| in DLO     : ${2*nP + 1}`);
    lines.push(`  realised in (Q,<) : ${real}   (the n algebraic types "x = aᵢ" plus the n+1 open-interval types)`);
    lines.push(`  omitted in (Q,<)  : ${cuts}   (Dedekind-cut types — saturated only after passing to an extension)`);
    lines.push('');
    if(nP === 0){
      lines.push('with no parameters, DLO has exactly one complete 1-type — every element looks the same.');
    } else {
      lines.push(`(Q,<) is ω-saturated for "open" types but omits cut types like x > a₁ ∧ x < a₂`);
      lines.push(`when no rational lies strictly between — but here density saves us, so all open intervals`);
      lines.push(`are realised. The cut types are limit-of-rationals types like "x = √2 from below."`);
    }

  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {

    const ctm = svg.getScreenCTM(); if(!ctm) return;

  clearBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ params=[]; render(); });
  demoBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ params=[2.5,4.0,7.5]; render(); });

5. Ehrenfeucht–Fraïssé games

Elementary equivalence is a yes/no question: do $\mathfrak{A},\mathfrak{B}$ agree on every first-order sentence? Ehrenfeucht–Fraïssé games refine it: how many quantifiers does it take to tell them apart?

The $n$-round EF game. Players are Spoiler and Duplicator, on $\sigma$-structures $\mathfrak{A},\mathfrak{B}$. Each round Spoiler picks an element from one structure; Duplicator must respond with an element from the other. After $n$ rounds Duplicator wins iff the resulting partial map (between the $2n$ chosen elements, paired by round) is a partial isomorphism — i.e. preserves all atomic relations.
EF theorem (Fraïssé 1954, Ehrenfeucht 1961). Duplicator has a winning strategy in the $n$-round EF game on $\mathfrak{A},\mathfrak{B}$ iff $\mathfrak{A}$ and $\mathfrak{B}$ agree on every first-order sentence of quantifier rank at most $n$. (Quantifier rank = maximum nesting depth of $\forall, \exists$.)

The proof is by induction on $n$, mirrored on both sides: a winning strategy is the witness that no rank-$n$ separator exists, and conversely.

Application: connectivity is not first-order. Take $\mathfrak{A}=C_{2n+1}$ (one cycle of length $2n+1$, connected) and $\mathfrak{B}=C_n\sqcup C_{n+1}$ (two cycles, disconnected). For $n$ rounds Duplicator can mirror Spoiler's moves locally — distances of order $\le 2^n$ are visible to a rank-$n$ formula, but the global topology isn't. So no rank-$n$ sentence distinguishes connected from disconnected for arbitrary $n$.

The widget below runs a 2-round EF game on two small linear orders. You play Spoiler against a fixed Duplicator strategy; if you can find a move sequence that traps Duplicator, you've discovered a rank-$\le 2$ sentence separating the two structures.

EF game: $(\{1,2,3\},<)$ vs $(\{1,2,3,4\},<)$
click an element; Duplicator copies; round-2 may trap her — that's a separator
round 0 of 2

<script>
/* =========================== EF game widget =========================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#ef-svg'), out=$('#ef-out');
  const resetBtn=$('#ef-reset'), roundLbl=$('#ef-round');
  // A = {1,2,3}, B = {1,2,3,4}
  const A = [1,2,3], B = [1,2,3,4];
  // record of moves: array of {side: 'A'|'B', val: number, byPlayer: 'S'|'D'}
  let moves = [];
  let verdict = null; // 'duplicator-wins' | 'spoiler-wins' | null

  function partialOK(){
    // collect S-D pairs
    const Apicks = []; // index in A
    const Bpicks = []; // index in B
    let i = 0;
    while(i < moves.length){
      const m1 = moves[i];
      const m2 = moves[i+1];
      if(!m2) break;
      if(m1.side === 'A'){ Apicks.push(m1.val); Bpicks.push(m2.val); }
      else { Bpicks.push(m1.val); Apicks.push(m2.val); }
      i += 2;
    }
    // check order-preserving
    for(let p=0;p<Apicks.length;p++) for(let q=0;q<Apicks.length;q++){
      if(p===q) continue;
      const ap = Apicks[p], aq = Apicks[q], bp = Bpicks[p], bq = Bpicks[q];
      if((ap<aq) !== (bp<bq)) return false;
      if((ap===aq) !== (bp===bq)) return false;
    }
    return true;
  }
  function duplicatorRespond(side, val){
    // a simple strategy: try to preserve order with all previously-paired elements
    // returns a value on the OTHER side, or null if no good response
    const opp = side==='A' ? 'B' : 'A';
    const pool = opp==='A' ? A : B;
    // collect existing pairs
    const pairs = []; // [{aval, bval}]
    let i=0;
    while(i<moves.length){
      const m1 = moves[i], m2 = moves[i+1];
      if(!m2) break;
      const ap = m1.side==='A' ? m1.val : m2.val;
      const bp = m1.side==='B' ? m1.val : m2.val;
      pairs.push({ap,bp});
      i+=2;
    }
    // find a pool value with the same order-pattern relative to existing pairs
    for(const cand of pool){
      let ok = true;
      for(const pr of pairs){
        const aRef = side==='A' ? pr.ap : pr.bp;       // partner on the same side as 'val'
        const bRef = opp==='A' ? pr.ap : pr.bp;        // partner on opposite side
        const sameOnVal = (val < aRef) ? '<' : (val > aRef ? '>' : '=');
        const sameOnCand = (cand < bRef) ? '<' : (cand > bRef ? '>' : '=');
        if(sameOnVal !== sameOnCand){ ok = false; break; }
      }
      // also check: cand must not equal any earlier opp-side pick paired with a different val (but pairs are distinct)
      if(ok) return cand;
    }
    return null;
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=240;
    const xL=80, xR=560;
    const yA=70, yB=170;
    function valToX(v, n){ return xL + (xR-xL)*(v-1)/(n-1); }
    // lines
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xL-10,y1:yA,x2:xR+10,y2:yA,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xL-10,y1:yB,x2:xR+10,y2:yB,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    const ttA = SVG('text',{x:30,y:yA+5,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--cyan)','font-weight':700});
    ttA.textContent='A'; svg.appendChild(ttA);
    const ttB = SVG('text',{x:30,y:yB+5,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--yellow)','font-weight':700});
    ttB.textContent='B'; svg.appendChild(ttB);
    // points
    A.forEach(v=>{
      const x = valToX(v,A.length);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x,cy:yA,r:8,fill:'var(--panel2)',stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.4,
        style: verdict?'':'cursor:pointer','data-side':'A','data-val':String(v)}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x,y:yA-14,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)','pointer-events':'none'});
      t.textContent = String(v); svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    B.forEach(v=>{
      const x = valToX(v,B.length);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x,cy:yB,r:8,fill:'var(--panel2)',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.4,
        style: verdict?'':'cursor:pointer','data-side':'B','data-val':String(v)}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x,y:yB+22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)','pointer-events':'none'});
      t.textContent = String(v); svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // moves overlaid: pair up consecutive moves
    let i=0; let r=0;
    while(i<moves.length){
      const m1 = moves[i], m2 = moves[i+1];
      const r1x = m1.side==='A' ? valToX(m1.val,A.length) : valToX(m1.val,B.length);
      const r1y = m1.side==='A' ? yA : yB;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:r1x,cy:r1y,r:11,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1.6}));
      if(m2){
        const r2x = m2.side==='A' ? valToX(m2.val,A.length) : valToX(m2.val,B.length);
        const r2y = m2.side==='A' ? yA : yB;
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:r2x,cy:r2y,r:11,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1.6}));
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:r1x,y1:r1y,x2:r2x,y2:r2y,
          stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.4,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
        const lbl = SVG('text',{x:(r1x+r2x)/2, y:(r1y+r2y)/2-2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--violet)'});
        lbl.textContent = `r${r+1}`; svg.appendChild(lbl);
        r++;
      }
      i += 2;
    }
    // verdict line
    if(verdict){
      const vt = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:H-20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,
        fill: verdict==='spoiler-wins'?'var(--pink)':'var(--green)', 'font-weight':700});
      vt.textContent = verdict==='spoiler-wins' ?
        '↯  Spoiler wins — partial map fails to preserve <  ⇒  ∃ rank-2 separator' :
        '✓  Duplicator survives 2 rounds — no rank-2 separator (yet)';
      svg.appendChild(vt);
    } else {
      const ht = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:H-20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      ht.textContent = `Spoiler's turn (round ${moves.length/2 + 1}): click any node`;
      svg.appendChild(ht);
    }
    roundLbl.textContent = `round ${Math.min(2, Math.floor(moves.length/2) + (moves.length%2? 0 : 0))} of 2`;
    if(moves.length===4 || verdict) roundLbl.textContent = `${moves.length>=4?2:Math.floor(moves.length/2)} of 2 rounds played`;
    renderReadout();
  }
  function renderReadout(){
    const lines = [];
    lines.push('moves so far:');
    if(moves.length===0) lines.push('  (none)');
    let r=0;
    for(let i=0;i<moves.length;i+=2){
      const m1 = moves[i], m2 = moves[i+1];
      r++;
      const s = `  round ${r}: Spoiler picks ${m1.val} on ${m1.side}`;
      const d = m2 ? `, Duplicator responds ${m2.val} on ${m2.side}` : '';
      lines.push(s + d);
    }
    lines.push('');
    if(verdict==='spoiler-wins'){
      lines.push('Duplicator could not preserve the < pattern.');
      lines.push('translation: there is a rank-2 sentence φ such that A ⊨ φ but B ⊭ φ.');
      lines.push('one such sentence: "∃x∃y(x<y ∧ ∀z(z<x ∨ z=x ∨ x<z<y ∨ z=y ∨ y<z))"');
      lines.push('— "there is an adjacent pair", which holds in {1,2,3} but the witness pattern in B is different.');
    } else if(verdict==='duplicator-wins'){
      lines.push('Duplicator survived 2 rounds.');
      lines.push('this only proves there is no rank-2 separator. It does not prove A ≡ B!');
      lines.push('larger games will eventually find a separator: A and B differ at rank ≈ log₂ of size.');
    } else {
      lines.push('after 2 full rounds we will have 4 moves total.');
      lines.push('then we check: does the order pattern between Spoiler\'s and Duplicator\'s picks match?');
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if(verdict) return;
    const side = e.target?.getAttribute?.('data-side');
    const valS = e.target?.getAttribute?.('data-val');
    if(!side || !valS) return;
    const val = parseInt(valS,10);
    // it must be Spoiler's turn (even-indexed move)
    if(moves.length % 2 !== 0) return;
    moves.push({side, val, byPlayer:'S'});
    // Duplicator responds
    const resp = duplicatorRespond(side, val);
    if(resp === null){
      // No legal response — Spoiler wins immediately
      verdict = 'spoiler-wins';
      render(); return;
    }
    moves.push({side: side==='A'?'B':'A', val: resp, byPlayer:'D'});
    if(moves.length >= 4){
      verdict = partialOK() ? 'duplicator-wins' : 'spoiler-wins';
    }
    render();
  });
  resetBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ moves=[]; verdict=null; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

6. Applications to algebra

Model theory's most striking consequences come from transfer principles: first-order facts can be moved between models of the same complete theory, even when the models look very different.

ACF$_p$ is complete. The theory of algebraically closed fields of fixed characteristic $p$ (for $p$ prime or $p=0$) is complete. Equivalently, any two algebraically closed fields of the same characteristic are elementarily equivalent. (Indeed, ACF$_p$ admits quantifier elimination — every formula reduces to a Boolean combination of polynomial equations.)
Lefschetz principle (model-theoretic version). A first-order sentence $\varphi$ in the language of rings holds in $\mathbb{C}$ iff it holds in $\overline{\mathbb{F}_p}$ for all but finitely many primes $p$.

The transfer is engineered by compactness: if $\varphi$ holds in $\mathbb{C}$ but fails in $\overline{\mathbb{F}_p}$ for infinitely many $p$, then the theory $\mathrm{ACF} \cup \{\varphi\} \cup \{p \neq 0 : p\text{ prime}\}$ is consistent (each finite subset has a model among $\overline{\mathbb{F}_q}$ for $q$ large), so by compactness it has a model — an algebraically closed field of characteristic $0$ where $\varphi$ fails — contradicting completeness of ACF$_0$.

Ax–Grothendieck

Ax–Grothendieck theorem (1968). Every injective polynomial map $f\colon\mathbb{C}^n\to\mathbb{C}^n$ is surjective.

The model-theoretic proof is two lines once you have the principle:

  1. For every fixed degree $d$, "every injective polynomial map of degree $\le d$ in $n$ variables is surjective" is a first-order sentence in the language of rings (you can encode polynomial identities up to that degree).
  2. Over $\overline{\mathbb{F}_p}$ this holds trivially: $\overline{\mathbb{F}_p}$ is a union of finite fields $\mathbb{F}_{p^k}$, and any injective map between finite sets is surjective.

Therefore the sentence holds in $\overline{\mathbb{F}_p}$ for all $p$. By Lefschetz it holds in $\mathbb{C}$. Done.

Why this is a thunderclap. An algebraic-geometry fact about $\mathbb{C}$ is reduced to the pigeonhole principle for finite sets, with model theory as the bridge. There is no analytic or topological input. The widget below walks the transfer step by step.

Ax–Grothendieck transfer
click a step to highlight; readout traces the chain of reductions

See also
  • Field extensions and minimal polynomials

(function(){

  const STEPS = [
    {ttl:'finite F_q',
     desc:'On any finite field F_q, an injective polynomial map F_q^n → F_q^n is surjective.',
     detail:'This is the pigeonhole principle: an injection between equally-sized finite sets is a bijection.'},
    {ttl:'∪ ⇒ alg.closure F̄_p',
     desc:'F̄_p = ⋃_{k≥1} F_{p^k}. Any polynomial map of fixed degree restricts to maps F_{p^k}^n → F_{p^k}^n for k large.',
     detail:'Therefore on F̄_p, every injective polynomial map is surjective: lift any "missed" point to a finite F_{p^k} where pigeonhole bites.'},
    {ttl:'first-order schema',
     desc:'For each n, d there is a first-order sentence φ_{n,d}: "every injective polynomial map of degree ≤d in n vars is surjective."',
     detail:'φ_{n,d} holds in F̄_p for every prime p (above).'},
    {ttl:'Lefschetz',
     desc:'ACF_0 and ACF_p (for varying p) form a complete theory at each characteristic. By compactness, anything true in F̄_p for all large p is true in C.',
     detail:'If φ_{n,d} failed in C, ACF_0 ∪ {¬φ_{n,d}} would be consistent — but every finite slice forces a positive-characteristic counterexample, contradiction.'},
    {ttl:'C',
     desc:'Hence φ_{n,d} holds in C for every n, d. ⇒ Ax–Grothendieck.',
     detail:'Every injective polynomial map C^n → C^n is surjective. ∎'}
  ];
  let cur = 0;

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const W=620, H=260;
    const padX=40;
    const stepW = (W - 2*padX) / STEPS.length;
    const cy = 100;
    for(let i=0;i<STEPS.length-1;i++){
      const x1 = padX + (i+0.5)*stepW;
      const x2 = padX + (i+1.5)*stepW;
      drawArrow(svg,[x1,cy],[x2,cy],{color:'var(--mute)',markerId:'ax-arr',width:1.2,pad1:38,pad2:38});
    }
    const labels = ['F_q','F̄_p','φ_{n,d}','Lefschetz','C'];
    for(let i=0;i<STEPS.length;i++){
      const x = padX + (i+0.5)*stepW;
      const isCur = i===cur;
      const fill = isCur ? 'rgba(255,216,102,0.18)' : 'var(--panel2)';
      const stroke = isCur ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--line)';
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:x,cy,r:30,fill,stroke,'stroke-width':isCur?2:1.2,
        style:'cursor:pointer','data-idx':String(i)}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x,y:cy+5,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,
        fill: isCur?'var(--yellow)':'#fff','font-weight':700,
        'font-family':'ui-monospace, monospace','pointer-events':'none'});
      t.textContent = labels[i];
      svg.appendChild(t);
      const lab = SVG('text',{x,y:cy+50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,
        fill:isCur?'var(--yellow)':'var(--mute)','pointer-events':'none'});
      lab.textContent = STEPS[i].ttl;
      svg.appendChild(lab);
    }
    const w = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:cy+100,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    w.textContent = `step ${cur+1} of ${STEPS.length}`;
    svg.appendChild(w);
    renderReadout();
  }
  function renderReadout(){
    const s = STEPS[cur];
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`step ${cur+1}: ${s.ttl}`);
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(s.desc);
    lines.push('');
    s.detail.split('\n').forEach(l=>lines.push('  ' + l));
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {
    const idx = e.target?.getAttribute?.('data-idx');
    if(idx==null) return;
    cur = parseInt(idx,10);
    render();
  });
  render();
})();
</script>
